steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: The text describes NASA's 2022 success in altering a moonlet's orbit, then pivots: scientists have not yet shown that primary objects like 2013 PY6 would be "similarly affected." The blank must signal doubt or opposition after a demonstrated success.

steps.title: Match the logical shift
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast/concession. The blank needs a transition signaling that the unproven claim about primary objects runs counter to the demonstrated success with moonlets.
  • Choice A — "for example,": signals a specific instance illustrating a general claim, but the primary-object question isn't an example of the moonlet success — it's a counterpoint. ✗
  • Choice B — "though,": signals contrast or qualification, so fits: NASA succeeded with moonlets, though that success hasn't been extended to primary objects. ✓
  • Choice C — "likewise,": signals similarity, but primary objects haven't been shown to behave like moonlets — that's exactly what's unproven. ✗
  • Choice D — "moreover,": signals an additional supporting point, but the unproven status of primary objects doesn't reinforce the moonlet success — it qualifies it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
